mechanism_narrative: The current level of the federal funds rate directly anchors short-term yields and influences the entire yield curve; a level near 3.88% signals moderately tight monetary policy that should keep upward pressure on the 10-year yield as investors demand compensation for holding longer-term bonds.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year rank correlation between FEDFUNDS level and subsequent 20-day yield change is below 0.05, or the sign of the correlation is negative in more than one subperiod.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment at 4.4% is near full employment; any further decline tightens the labor market, raising wage pressures and inflation expectations, which forces the bond market to price in higher future short rates and pushes the 10-year yield up.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE triggers, the median 20-day forward yield change is not positive, or the proportion of positive moves is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and retail sales confirms synchronized demand-side strength, which boosts real GDP growth expectations and leads investors to demand higher yields to compensate for increased economic activity and potential inflation.
failure_condition: When the joint positive-growth condition is satisfied, the 20-day forward yield change is negative more than 40% of the time, or the average magnitude of positive moves is less than that of negative moves.

mechanism_narrative: With CPI at 324.368 (significantly above its pre-pandemic trend), the economy is in a high-inflation regime; any further increase in CPI reinforces expectations that the Fed will keep rates elevated or hike again, directly lifting the 10-year yield as term premiums rise.
failure_condition: While CPI > 320, the 20-day forward yield change is not positively correlated with CPI level surprises; rolling 2-year Spearman rank correlation falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 101.43 indicates stable output; a rising INDPRO level signals robust supply capacity but also demand pressures, and over a 60-day horizon the persistent strength in production is a reliable proxy for economic momentum that pushes yields upward.
failure_condition: Rolling 5-year rank correlation between INDPRO level and subsequent 60-day yield change is below 0.03, or the sign is negative in a majority of 2-year subwindows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts of 1307 thousand are moderate; if starts accelerate above the 1500 level, it signals a booming housing market that strongly boosts residential investment and overall demand, quickly feeding into higher yields over a short 5-day horizon as cyclical momentum is priced in.
failure_condition: On days when HOUST > 1500, the 5-day forward yield change is negative more than 45% of the time, or the average positive return is not statistically different from zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll growth exceeds 200k while the fed funds rate remains at 3.88% (unchanged from the previous month), the combination signals a resilient labor market without immediate policy tightening; this increases inflation risk premiums and pushes the 10-year yield higher over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 200 and FEDFUNDS unchanged from prior month
failure_condition: Whenever the joint condition holds, the 20-day forward yield change is positive less than 60% of the time, or the mean return is negative in a bootstrapped hypothesis test (p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment regime (UNRATE below 4.5%), the labor market is historically tight; under this condition, any positive payroll surprise directly boosts wage pressure and inflation expectations, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Within the low-unemployment regime, the Spearman rank correlation between PAYEMS growth and subsequent 20-day yield change is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or the average yield change in the top quintile of payroll growth is not significantly different from the bottom quintile.
